---
title: "SL_final_proj"
author: "Vanessa Lattas"
date: "2025-04-28"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

rm(list = ls()) #Clear workspace

library("tidyverse")          #For data manipulation
library("ggplot2")            #For nice plots
library('Metrics')
library('gridExtra')
library('dplyr')
library("ISLR")
library("glmnet")
library("MASS")
library("caret")
library("splines")
library("boot")
library("class")
library("nnet")
library('Rmpfr')
library('reshape2')
library('pROC')
```

```{r load data}

data <- read.csv("~/Desktop/train.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ",")
# converting categorical variables
data$Sex <- factor(data$Sex)
data$Sex <- as.numeric(data$Sex)

data$Diet <- factor(data$Diet)
data$Diet <- as.numeric(data$Diet)

data$Country <- factor(data$Country)
data$Country <- as.numeric(data$Country)

data$Continent <- factor(data$Continent)
data$Continent <- as.numeric(data$Continent)

data$Hemisphere <- factor(data$Hemisphere)
data$Hemisphere <- as.numeric(data$Hemisphere)

data$Stress.Level <- factor(data$Stress.Level)
data$Stress.Level <- as.numeric(data$Stress.Level)

# converting blood pressure to usable values
data <- data %>%
  separate(Blood.Pressure, into = c("systolic", "diastolic"), sep = "/") %>%
  mutate(across(c(systolic, diastolic), as.numeric))

# converting int to num
data <- data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.integer), as.numeric))

# check for NaNs
NaNs = colSums(is.na(data))
# no missing values 

# remove sub ID 
clean_data <- subset(data, select = -Patient.ID)

# Load libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Plot Age Distribution
ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = Age, fill = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk))) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "stack", color = "black") +
  labs(
       x = "Age", y = "Count",
       fill = "Heart Attack Risk") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray", "red"), labels = c("No Risk", "At Risk"))


# Plot Heart Attack Risk by Sex
ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = as.factor(Sex), fill = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Heart Attack Risk by Sex", x = "Sex", y = "Proportion") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray", "red"), name = "Heart Attack Risk") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("1" = "Male", "2" = "Female"))

# Plot BMI vs Age Colored by Risk
ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = Age, y = BMI, color = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  labs( x = "Age", y = "BMI") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("blue", "red"), name = "Heart Attack Risk")
library(ggplot2)

# Plot BMI by Heart Attack Risk
ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk), y = BMI, fill = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk))) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, color = "black") +
  labs(x = "Heart Attack Risk",
       y = "BMI",
       fill = "Heart Attack Risk") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray", "red"),
                    labels = c("0" = "No Risk", "1" = "At Risk")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"  # remove redundant legend
  )


# Plot Heart Attack Risk by Physical Activity
ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk), y = Physical.Activity.Days.Per.Week)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue") +
  labs(title = "Physical Activity vs Heart Attack Risk", x = "Heart Attack Risk", y = "Activity Days per Week")


# Plot Heart Attack Risk by Country
ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = as.factor(Country), fill = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(x = "Country", y = "Proportion") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray", "red"), name = "Heart Attack Risk")

# Plot Heart Attack Risk by Smoking Status
ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = as.factor(Smoking), fill = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(x = "Smoking (0 = No, 1 = Yes)",
       y = "Proportion") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray", "red"), name = "Heart Attack Risk")

# Plot Heart Attack Risk by Diabetes Status
ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = as.factor(Diabetes), fill = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(x = "Diabetes (0 = No, 1 = Yes)",
       y = "Proportion") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray", "red"), name = "Heart Attack Risk")

# Plot Heart Attack Risk by Physical Activity
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = Physical.Activity.Days.Per.Week, fill = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk))) +
  geom_histogram(position = "dodge", bins = 15, color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "Physical Activity Days Per Week",
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Heart Attack Risk") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray", "red"),
                    labels = c("0" = "0", "1" = "1")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# Plot Heart Attack Risk by Obesity status
ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = as.factor(Obesity), fill = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(x = "Obesity (0 = No, 1 = Yes)", y = "Proportion") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray", "red"), name = "Heart Attack Risk")

# Plot Heart Attack Risk by Family History
ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = as.factor(Family.History), fill = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(x = "Family History (0 = No, 1 = Yes)", y = "Proportion") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray", "red"), name = "Heart Attack Risk")


# Boxplot of Cholesterol by Heart Attack Risk
ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk), y = Cholesterol, fill = as.factor(Heart.Attack.Risk))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(x = "Heart Attack Risk (0 = No, 1 = Yes)", y = "Cholesterol Level") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray", "red"), name = "Heart Attack Risk") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```

SMOTE TO HANDLE CLASS IMBALANCE

```{r}
install.packages("themis")
library(themis)
library(recipes)

set.seed(123)

cat("\n Class distribution BEFORE SMOTE:\n")
print(table(clean_data$Heart.Attack.Risk))
print(prop.table(table(clean_data$Heart.Attack.Risk)))

# Ensure response is factor
clean_data$Heart.Attack.Risk <- factor(clean_data$Heart.Attack.Risk)

# Create a recipe with SMOTE
recipe_balanced <- recipe(Heart.Attack.Risk ~ ., data = clean_data) %>%
  step_smote(Heart.Attack.Risk, over_ratio = 0.85)  

balanced_data <- prep(recipe_balanced) %>% bake(new_data = NULL)
table(balanced_data$Heart.Attack.Risk)  # Check balance

cat("\n Class distribution AFTER SMOTE:\n")
print(table(balanced_data$Heart.Attack.Risk))
print(prop.table(table(balanced_data$Heart.Attack.Risk)))


```

SPLIT TRAIN/TEST

```{r}
# Load required packages
library(randomForest)
library(caret)
library(themis)
library(recipes)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(123)

# Split data into training and testing sets (80/20 split)
train_index <- createDataPartition(balanced_data$Heart.Attack.Risk, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- balanced_data[train_index, ]
test_data <- balanced_data[-train_index, ]

```

RANDOM FOREST

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(randomForest)
library(caret)
library(pROC)

# Fit random forest model
rf_model <- randomForest(
  Heart.Attack.Risk ~ ., 
  data = train_data,
  importance=TRUE)

# Print model summary
print(rf_model)

# Make predictions on test set
rf_pred <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data)

# Confusion matrix
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(rf_pred, test_data$Heart.Attack.Risk, positive = "1")
print(conf_matrix)
# Extract matrix and reshape for ggplot
cm_table <- as.table(conf_matrix$table)
cm_df <- as.data.frame(cm_table)
colnames(cm_df) <- c("True", "Predicted", "Freq")

# Plot using ggplot2
ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Predicted, y = True, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), size = 8) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix", fill = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  coord_fixed()

## ----------------------------
## Compute F1 Score (manual)
## ----------------------------

tp <- conf_matrix$table["1", "1"]
fp <- conf_matrix$table["0", "1"]
fn <- conf_matrix$table["1", "0"]

precision <- tp / (tp + fp)
recall <- tp / (tp + fn)
f1_rf <- ifelse((precision + recall) == 0, 0, 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall))

cat("Random Forest F1 Score (manual):", f1_rf, "\n")

## ----------------------------
## ROC Curve and AUC
## ----------------------------

# Get predicted probabilities for class "1"
rf_probs <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")[, "1"]

roc_obj <- roc(test_data$Heart.Attack.Risk, rf_probs)
plot(roc_obj, main = "ROC Curve for Random Forest")

cat("Random Forest AUC:", auc(roc_obj), "\n")

## ----------------------------
## Variable Importance Plot
## ----------------------------

varImpPlot(rf_model, 
           main = "Variable Importance Plot",
           cex = 0.8)        # Adjust text size if needed

```

K MEANS CLUSTERING, K MEDOIDS

```{r}
library(caret)
library(cluster)
library(pROC)
library(MLmetrics)
library(dplyr)

# Drop non-numeric (categorical) variables for clustering
exclude_vars <- c("Heart.Attack.Risk", "Sex", "Hemisphere", "Continent", "Country", "Diet")
kmeans_data <- balanced_data[, !(names(balanced_data) %in% exclude_vars)]
str(kmeans_data)

# Scale numeric data
kmeans_data <- scale(kmeans_data)

# Ensure true labels are 0/1 factor
true_labels <- as.numeric(as.character(balanced_data$Heart.Attack.Risk))
true_labels <- factor(true_labels, levels = c(0, 1))

# ==========================
# K-MEANS CLASSIFIER
# ==========================

set.seed(123)
kmeans_model <- kmeans(kmeans_data, centers = 2, nstart = 25)

# Cluster-to-label table
cluster_map <- table(kmeans_model$cluster, true_labels)
cat("\n K-means cluster-to-label mapping:\n")
print(cluster_map)

# Determine best global mapping
agreement1 <- cluster_map[1, "0"] + cluster_map[2, "1"]
agreement2 <- cluster_map[1, "1"] + cluster_map[2, "0"]

if (agreement1 > agreement2) {
  cluster_to_class <- c("0", "1")
} else {
  cluster_to_class <- c("1", "0")
}

# Predict classes
kmeans_pred <- factor(cluster_to_class[kmeans_model$cluster], levels = c("0", "1"))

# Confusion Matrix for K-means
cm_kmeans <- confusionMatrix(kmeans_pred, true_labels, positive = "1")
cat("\n K-means Accuracy:", cm_kmeans$overall["Accuracy"], "\n")
print(cm_kmeans)
# Extract matrix and reshape for ggplot
cm_table <- as.table(cm_kmeans$table)
cm_df <- as.data.frame(cm_table)
colnames(cm_df) <- c("True", "Predicted", "Freq")

# Plot using ggplot2
ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Predicted, y = True, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), size = 8) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix", fill = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  coord_fixed()



# Compute F1 Score
f1_kmeans <- F1_Score(y_pred = kmeans_pred, y_true = true_labels, positive = "1")
cat("\n K-means F1 Score (positive = 1):", f1_kmeans, "\n")

# ROC curve and AUC
kmeans_prob <- as.numeric(kmeans_pred == "1")
roc_kmeans <- roc(as.numeric(as.character(true_labels)), kmeans_prob)
plot(roc_kmeans, main = "K-means ROC Curve")
cat(" K-means AUC:", auc(roc_kmeans), "\n")


```

K MEDOIDS

```{r}
# ==========================
# K-MEDOIDS CLASSIFIER (performed worse, did NOT report in paper)
# ==========================

set.seed(123)
kmedoids_model <- pam(kmeans_data, k = 2)

# Cluster-to-label table
cluster_map_med <- table(kmedoids_model$clustering, true_labels)
cat("\n K-medoids cluster-to-label mapping:\n")
print(cluster_map_med)

# Determine best global mapping
agreement1_med <- cluster_map_med[1, "0"] + cluster_map_med[2, "1"]
agreement2_med <- cluster_map_med[1, "1"] + cluster_map_med[2, "0"]

if (agreement1_med > agreement2_med) {
  cluster_to_class_med <- c("0", "1")
} else {
  cluster_to_class_med <- c("1", "0")
}

# Predict classes
kmedoids_pred <- factor(cluster_to_class_med[kmedoids_model$clustering], levels = c("0", "1"))

# Confusion Matrix for K-medoids
cm_kmedoids <- confusionMatrix(kmedoids_pred, true_labels, positive = "1")
cat("\n K-medoids Accuracy:", cm_kmedoids$overall["Accuracy"], "\n")
print(cm_kmedoids)

# Compute F1 Score
f1_kmedoids <- F1_Score(y_pred = kmedoids_pred, y_true = true_labels, positive = "1")
cat("\n K-medoids F1 Score (positive = 1):", f1_kmedoids, "\n")

# ROC curve and AUC
kmedoids_prob <- as.numeric(kmedoids_pred == "1")
roc_kmedoids <- roc(as.numeric(as.character(true_labels)), kmedoids_prob)
plot(roc_kmedoids, main = "K-medoids ROC Curve")
cat("K-medoids AUC:", auc(roc_kmedoids), "\n")


```

NAIVE BAYES

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(e1071)       # For Naive Bayes
library(caret)       # For confusion matrix
library(pROC)        # For ROC curve

# non-ordered categorical predictors, don't want to treat them as numeric here
balanced_data$Country <- factor(balanced_data$Country)
balanced_data$Hemisphere <- factor(balanced_data$Hemisphere)
balanced_data$Continent <- factor(balanced_data$Hemisphere)
balanced_data$Diet <- factor(balanced_data$Diet)

## ----------------------------
## Train Naive Bayes Model
## ----------------------------

# Train model with Laplace smoothing
nb_model <- naiveBayes(
  Heart.Attack.Risk ~ ., 
  data = train_data,
  laplace = 1          # Prevents zero probabilities
)

# View model summary
print(nb_model)

## ----------------------------
## Make Predictions
## ----------------------------

# Predict class probabilities
nb_probs <- predict(nb_model, newdata = test_data, type = "raw")

# Predict class labels
nb_pred <- predict(nb_model, newdata = test_data)

## ----------------------------
## Evaluate Performance
## ----------------------------

# Confusion matrix
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(
  nb_pred,
  test_data$Heart.Attack.Risk,
  positive = "1"       # Assuming "1" is the positive class (heart attack risk)
)

# Print detailed metrics
print(conf_matrix)

# Extract matrix and reshape for ggplot
cm_table <- as.table(conf_matrix$table)
cm_df <- as.data.frame(cm_table)
colnames(cm_df) <- c("True", "Predicted", "Freq")

# Plot using ggplot2
ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Predicted, y = True, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), size = 8) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix", fill = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  coord_fixed()

## ----------------------------
## Compute F1 Score
## ----------------------------

# Method 2: Manual F1 calculation
tp <- conf_matrix$table["1", "1"]
fp <- conf_matrix$table["0", "1"]
fn <- conf_matrix$table["1", "0"]

precision <- tp / (tp + fp)
recall <- tp / (tp + fn)
f1_nb <- ifelse((precision + recall) == 0, 0, 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall))

cat("Naive Bayes F1 Score (manual):", f1_nb, "\n")

## ----------------------------
## ROC Curve (requires probability predictions)
## ----------------------------

roc_obj <- roc(test_data$Heart.Attack.Risk, nb_probs[, "1"])
plot(roc_obj, main = "ROC Curve for Naive Bayes")
cat("Naive Bayes AUC:", auc(roc_obj), "\n")

```
